title: 4/23/20 Turn comments back on YouTube! FOR KIDS

transcript.text: Hi, Livin' in Boulder, V-LOG, April 23rd, 2020, 523 a.m. Thursday.
Um, I, uh, figured I'd do my video now, cause it's 523, it's daytime.
Sun hadn't started to come out yet, but, uh, maybe I should lie down.
I'm purger seen, I've been working.
Um, yesterday I was spending most of the day doing rather boring stuff on my ancient history timeline.
Making sure that all of the, um, stages, ages, epochs, periods, and all that stuff are on the vertical part of my timeline.
It was on the, uh, the horizontal, and then I was having to convert a lot of, uh, other stuff, cause a lot of the ages were using, um, the battle.
I call it, I got all my CSS designs from my main website, that's why they're called from a regular history timeline.
But I changed it from battles to culture because it was a little bit bigger font, and I already used battle.
Um, or battle looks just like, um, country, and I used that for the, uh, animals, so.
A lot of times you don't know, or I don't know, um, which CSS, which font I want to use.
Until I get a bunch of fonts up there for both the words and for the, uh, borders.
Uh, for example, right now I'm not, I'm wondering if I want to have the dotted war font, or maybe it's a battle font, uh, instead of just the solid, uh, country font.
Uh, uh, um, yeah, you figure that stuff out just naturally, you get convinced of something.
Um, but, um, it kind of took forever, just making sure everything was aligned, you know.
I was just, just, just racing, trying to fix it, really.
Make it look, um, consistent.
And then over the last hour or so, I've been, I've been putting dinosaurs, preparing dinosaurs to put them on my timeline.
I've got a bunch of dinosaurs already, but I didn't get a complete list or a semi-complete list.

I decided to get a list of dinosaurs from this video called Walking with Dinosaurs, which somebody made an animation of a person walking and then different dinosaurs going past them to show what they look like and how big they are.
And I just, they, they had the list of dinosaurs at the bottom of the video.
And, um, so I just copied that and then I'm putting the information.
So I'm doing the dates of the range of the dinosaurs and then I'm writing where they were found, what part of the world they live.
I definitely want to know all that stuff.
And then as far as identifying whether it's a vegetarian or a carnivore, I just do that color-codedly.
That's what all the color-coding and font-coding is for.
Save space.
Save space.
So, yeah, after I do these dinosaurs and plug them into my timeline, I'm going to, um, do the same thing.
Because the person did a video for, like, Cenozoic, you know, animals, animals from, um, the time after the dinosaurs.
All the giant beasts, you know, mammoths and stuff.
So I'll get a bunch of those, throw those on, all the different species.
And after I throw it on, you know, I'll see how it looks, you know, because there's a whole bunch of them.
But if you spread them out over time, maybe they won't have to overlap and you won't have to have a timeline that's too long.
You know, I'm dealing with a little bit of an issue with my timeline from 500 BC to 1000 BC South Europe with Rome.
Because I included all of the different tribes that were at the time of Rome and it just goes on forever.
And I'm, I think, I think I need to, um, consolidate that by putting all the different, you know, tribes that existed at the same time all together.
That's what I, that's the one thing that I'm going to do.
I, I, I'm a, I'm almost perfect in my, my timeline design.
I almost get it right the first time every time, but not always, you know.
That's the key is to try to get stuff right earliest on as possible so that you don't have to redo stuff and keep redoing stuff.
There are a bunch of people who's, there's no way in hell they could do what I do.
You could make a timeline like mine because they'd never be, they'd never be decisive enough, you know, and then they just give up.
You know, they do a little bit and they'd be like, oh no, it needs to do, oh no, it needs to, and then they just give up.
So with me, I know, and then I go for it, you know.
Uh, and then I do micro adjustments after that.
So that's how my timeline works.
You know, I, I, um, I, um, I, uh, look at it, you know, I built it based, I built it based on how can I get the most information in the smallest space.
And then as I look at it, I just, if something bothers me, you know, and something's not functioning right, then that's when I fix it.
I, I have the don't fix it unless it's broke mentality.
And, uh, I know if something's broken just by looking at it, you know, maybe that's, that's one reason I like to smoke weed.
You know, you, I like to switch up my mind when I look at things so that when I look at my timeline, like it covers all bases.
And there's absolutely nothing that I would ever change about my timeline.
That's when I know it's done.

Um, and then that's when I would then explain it, why I made all of the different decisions, you know, and then invite people to critique the hell out of my timeline because whoever critiques me gets critiqued.
I get to critique the hell out of your critique.
And that's where the fun starts.
So, uh, nobody's critiquing my timeline.
I know nobody has anything to say about my, my whole website, whether it's positive or negative.
I think the jury, the jury's just still out and it's pretty much, it's pretty much common knowledge that the jury's out.
So nobody messes with me and I don't mess with anybody and I don't mess with myself.
I just keep working.
Um, but, um, I, uh, what day is it?
Um, I guess it's Thursday morning.
I gave, I told myself, I think I started like last Saturday.
So I gave myself until this Sunday, one week.
I already know by the time I get to Sunday, it's going to be worlds different.

Ancient history timeline is going to be worlds different than when it was a week before.

I don't think I don't have anything wrong with me.
